description: Made with 65% Chardonnay, 15% Sauvignon, 10% Pinot Grigio and 10% Pinot Bianco, this elegant white offers aromas of white flower, tropical fruit, mint and whiffs of toasted oak. The structured palate delivers Golden Delicious apple, vanilla, oak and lemon zest. A mineral note closes the lingering finish.
title: Cantina Produttori San Michele Appiano 2012 Appius White (Alto Adige)

description: The nose is blowsy and dusty, with waxy white-fruit aromas. The palate is juicy and fresh but doesn't offer much body. Finishing flavors of nectarine, papaya and orange are basic.
title: Ressò 2012 Old Vines Garnacha Blanca (Catalunya)

description: A distinctive wine that comes from a biodynamically farmed area of this vineyard, with 40% fermented in neutral oak and the rest in stainless steel. It's brightly aromatic with ripe peach, honeysuckle and a spritz of lime, smelling quite luscious but drinking dry with a broad feel to the lively fruit flavors.
title: Beast 2012 Riesling (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: This wine is half Cabernet Sauvignon, with the balance Merlot (20%), Cabernet Franc (18%) and a healthy dollop of Petit Verdot. Aromas of green herbs, fresh flowers, soil and barrel spice lead to dark cherry flavors backed by grainy tannins.
title: :Nota Bene 2012 Arianses Vineyard Red (Wahluke Slope)

description: Owned by the Hessel family since the 15th century, this estate has produced a dense and solid Merlot-dominated wine. It is big and bold with ripe berry fruits and dark tannins. The black plum skin and berry flavors are concentrated and firm. It needs to age, so drink from 2018.
title: Château des Annereaux 2012  Lalande de Pomerol

description: Oaky aromas of wood grain, graphite, bacon, rubber and asphalt blend with ripe blackberry scents to create an inky, potent bouquet. This feels round and lush, with cutting lemony acidity. Flavors of baked blackberry, cassis and chocolate finish ripe, healthy and toasty. Drink through 2019.
title: Tritón 2012  Toro

description: Intensely smoky and earthy in style, this wild-ferment Riesling is strikingly dry with pressed apple and pear flavors struck with lime acidity. Lean and linear, it's bristling and tart to the finish.
title: Bellwether 2012 A&D Vineyard Wild Ferment Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: It invigorates the senses with concentrated lemon and lime flavors tinged with minerals, tart stone fruits and blossoms. It's dry in style with a bold, mouth-filling palate, but gorgeously elegant. Finishes long with a ripple of lime-zest astringency.
designation: Magdalena Vineyard Dry Estate Bottled and Grown
title: Hermann J. Wiemer 2012 Magdalena Vineyard Dry Estate Bottled and Grown Riesling (Seneca Lake)

description: Charred earth, smoke, roasted coffee bean and raisin aromas come together on this. The brawny palate offers prune, blackberry jam, licorice and mocha alongside tightly packed tannins that leave a drying, rather astringent finish.
title: Palazzo Maffei 2012  Amarone della Valpolicella

description: This is an approachable, affordable wine that impresses on every level. It combines raspberry, vanilla and plum flavors with juicy, moderate acidity and rounded texture, the fruit accented with clove, cedar and bay leaf notes.
title: Del Carlo Winery 2012 Teldeschi Vineyards Home Ranch Cabernet Sauvignon (Dry Creek Valley)

description: With aromas of wood aging, this is a tropical fruit and spice flavored wine. It displays a strong sense of texture and freshness. It is already showing richness as well as a good sense of style. Drink from 2015.
title: Quinta do Quetzal 2012 Guadalupe Selection Branco Antão Vaz (Alentejano)

description: The vineyard is on Westside Road, in the warmer part of the valley. The wine is gentle, balanced and immediately likeable for its array of raspberries, cherries and cola. Heat from alcohol makes it a little ungainly, and may limit ageability. Drink through 2019.
title: Gracianna 2012 Mercedes Riverblock Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Minty and bright, this is a well-distributed Pinot that's a fair value, soft and easy to drink. Amidst mellowed oak, expect dark cherry and buoyed acidity with a finish of darker, earthy tones.
title: Sebastiani 2012 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Tight, light and layered in freshly baked bread and firm cherry and cranberry, this is a soft, rounded Pinot from an inland Sonoma Coast site that's balanced, food-friendly and ready to enjoy now through 2016.
title: Armida 2012 Parmalee-Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: With significant amounts of Mourvèdre (15%) and Grenache (8%) blended in, this drinks more like a GSM than a Syrah. Cranberry, herb and woodspice aromas are followed by tart, sleek fruit and smoke flavors that linger.
title: :Nota Bene 2012 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Charred blue- and black-berry aromas mingle with cola and coffee on the nose. The palate follows suit, adding in a toasted black pepper spice that leads to a mild burnt-bacon finish. Medium bodied with prickling acidity, this toasty, gamy Syrah could sit through 2016 to mellow out.
title: Mackinaw Trail 2012 Unrestricted Syrah (Michigan)

description: An enormously fruity wine, it's impressive in Meyer lemons, limes, apricots, mangoes and vanilla, succulent and savory, and brightened by acidity. With almost the weight of an oak-aged Chardonnay, it shouldn't be opened too cold. Pair it with everything from ham and sausages and broiled chicken to arroz con pollo and Thai pork and peanut curry.
title: Envy 2012 Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Calistoga)

description: Rich in grenadine and orange peel flavor, Red Electra is infused with the perfume of jasmine. Slightly effervescent, it would be delightful over ice or mixed into a gin and tonic and at under 6% alcohol is a nice apéritif to serve any guest.
title: Quady 2012 Red Electra Moscato (California)

description: Generous flavors with a full body add up to a very likable, mouthfilling wine that will love a juicy steak or grilled lamb chop. Almost-sweet spices like vanilla and nutmeg come out in the aroma and persist through the fresh black cherry flavors and finish.
title: Tortoise Creek 2012 Cherokee Lane Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)

description: This opens with subdued aromas of French wood, grilled herb, forest floor and dark spice. The firmly structured palate shows espresso, toast, powdered sage and licorice but not much fruit richness. Grainy drying tannins leave an astringent mouthfeel.
title: La Spinetta 2012 Vigneto Bordini  (Barbaresco)

description: Funky aromas recall barnyard, wet earth and overripe black fruit. The rustic palate offers mature blackberry and gamy notes alongside fleeting tannins.
title: Feudi del Pisciotto 2012 Versace Nero d'Avola (Sicilia)

description: A blend of fruit from Boushey, Ciel du Cheval and Seven Hills vineyards, this wine opens with aromas of plum, Asian spices, licorice, dark fruit and light barrel spices. The palate is rich, full bodied and velvety, with abundant coffee flavors and a mesmerizing sense of texture.
title: Maison Bleue 2012 Bourgeois Grenache (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Holding 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Cabernet Franc and 10% Merlot, this wine offers brooding aromas of fresh herbs, cherry, mocha and vanilla. The palate displays pleasing density and weight to the cranberry and cherry flavors that are accented by bright, zippy acidity.
title: Chandler Reach 2012 Monte Regalo Red (Yakima Valley)

description: A blend of Klipsun and Obelisco vineyards, this Cabernet Sauvignon leads with aromas of blackberry jam, espresso, fresh herbs and toasty barrel spices (60% new French). The black fruit flavors are rich, ripe and sweet, with the oak playing a prominent part in the show.
title: Guardian 2012 The Rookie Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Mountain)

description: Aromas of woodspice, cedar, cranberry and cherry are at the fore on this 100% varietal wine. It shows concentration, finesse and polish, with dry, chewy tannins lending support.
title: Obelisco Estate 2012 Nefer III Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Mountain)

description: This extremely flavorful but young and tightly wound wine has black pepper, dark chocolate and smoke aromas, concentrated blackberry and cassis flavors, firm tannins and full body. This is a powerful, full-bodied, firmly structured wine that needs time to show its best side. It should drink beautifully after 2018.
title: Obsidian Ridge 2012 Estate Syrah (Red Hills Lake County)

description: This blend of Cabernet Franc and Gamay concentrates on fruit and soft acidity. It has some smokiness and rounded tannins, fresh and full of red fruits. It is ready to drink.
title: Domaine Joël Delaunay 2012 Domaine de la Brossette Red (Touraine)

description: This light-colored and easy-drinking wine offers bright cherry and tea aromas followed by raspberry/cranberry flavors on a crisp texture with good acidity and moderate tannins. It shows the leaner side of Pinot.
title: Drew 2012 Weir Vineyard Pinot Noir (Yorkville Highlands)

description: Break out the prosciutto-wrapped figs and stuffed mushroom appetizers for this light, silky rosé. It's crisp in acidity and delicate in the mouth, with assertive raspberry, orange tea and honey flavors. This is one of the more successful Pinot Noir rosés out there.
title: Festival and Feast 2012 Rosé of Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)

description: This bottling, which is heavily bolstered by a 22% addition of Petite Sirah, shows aromas of chocolate, blueberry and fresh violets, with a leathery backbone. It's lavish and soft on the palate, rich with fudge and espresso notes and ripe with blueberry sauce.
title: Myka 2012 Kane Teague Vineyards Cabernet Sauvignon (San Benito County)

description: This single-vineyard wine is always dense and powerful. This edition opens with prune, blackberry, Graham cracker and earthy aromas. In the mouth, it's pinched, with rubbery tannins. Salty, roasted flavors are true to the Salta region, while the finish is black in flavor and bulky in feel.
title: Michel Torino 2012 Don David Finca La Maravilla #6 Malbec (Cafayate)

description: Leather, underbrush, earth, iris and woodland berry are some of the scents you'll discover on this structured wine. The concentrated palate delivers tart red cherry, licorice, cinnamon and sage before leading into a juicy finish. Firm tannins offer support. Drink 2018–2026.
title: Poderi Luigi Einaudi 2012 Cannubi  (Barolo)

description: Dark-skinned berry, Mediterranean herb and forest floor aromas slowly emerge along with a balsamic note. The palate offers dried black cherry, mocha, vanilla and a hint of oak alongside fine-grained tannins that leave a firm finish.
title: Barone di Villagrande 2012 Rosso Villagrande  (Etna)

description: Hints of crushed stones lend a mineral focus to this dry, elegantly structured wine. It's piercingly tart on the palate with loads of fresh lime and lemon flavor, but gains richness on the midpalate with streaks of fresh apple and crisp yellow peach. A hint of saffron adds spice to the moderately long finish.
title: Damiani 2012 Davis Vineyard Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: An aromatically appealing wine with notes of Granny Smith apples, peach and herbs. It drinks medium sweet with jasmine and stone fruit flavors.
title: Latah Creek 2012 Riesling (Ancient Lakes)

description: This inky Malbec opens with an oaky blend of vanilla, blackberry, prune, cassis and tobacco aromas. A compact, firm palate is just lush enough, while oak-based flavors of cinnamon and baking spices rest on top of spiced plum and cassis. A peppery finish is intermixed with tobacco notes. Drink through 2021.
title: Penedo Borges 2012 Icono Malbec (Luján de Cuyo)

description: Classic Sangiovese aromas of red berry, culinary spice, crushed herb, tobacco and a whiff of new leather float from the glass. The juicy full-bodied palate delivers fleshy wild cherry, raspberry compote, vanilla and star anise framed in firm, refined tannins. Fresh acidity lends balance. Drink 2019–2027.
title: La Mannella 2012  Brunello di Montalcino
